# SEO Content UX Playbook

## What This Skill Does
This skill creates practical, production-ready content plans that connect:
- SEO intent and discoverability
- Humanized, trustworthy copy
- UX clarity and conversion flow
- Frontend-ready content structure

Default language and tone:
- Spanish (LatAm)
- Professional and clear
- Balanced SEO and readability

## When To Use
- New landing pages, feature pages, pricing pages, service pages, and blog content hubs
- Rewriting robotic content into natural, useful copy
- Aligning UX flows with organic search intent
- Preparing content that developers can implement directly

## Inputs To Request
- Business goal of the page
- User intent type (informational, commercial, transactional, navigational)
- Primary keyword and secondary topics/entities
- Product constraints and brand tone
- Target route or URL

## Procedure
1. Define search intent and conversion goal:
- Confirm one primary goal and one primary intent.
- List top user questions and objections.

2. Build content architecture:
- Draft H1, H2, H3 structure.
- Map each section to intent stage and CTA.
- Add FAQ only if it resolves real friction.

3. Draft humanized copy:
- Use concrete language, short paragraphs, and varied sentence rhythm.
- Avoid keyword stuffing, generic filler, and exaggerated claims.
- Keep copy scannable and useful first.

4. Build SEO metadata pack:
- Provide title tag, meta description, slug suggestion, canonical note, and internal linking targets.
- Suggest schema type only when meaningful (FAQPage, Product, Article, Organization, BreadcrumbList).

5. Add UX writing layer:
- Rewrite CTA text, helper text, and empty states for clarity.
- Reduce cognitive load with plain language and strong action verbs.

6. Translate to frontend handoff:
- Return section-by-section copy blocks ready for components.
- Include content priorities for mobile first.

## Output Template
1. Goal and search intent
2. Keyword and entity map
3. Content outline (H1-H3)
4. Humanized copy draft
5. Metadata pack (title/meta/slug/canonical/schema)
6. UX writing improvements (CTA, helper text, microcopy)
7. Frontend handoff notes (component-ready blocks)
8. Validation checklist (SEO, readability, accessibility)

## Quality Guardrails
- Keep claims accurate and verifiable.
- Prefer clarity over clever phrasing.
- Ensure inclusive and accessible language.
- Do not generate manipulative or spam tactics.
